per_camera.front_left: Frames are almost entirely white/washed out with faint blue artifacts; camera appears severely overexposed or malfunctioning, providing no usable scene information.
per_camera.front_right: Shows a stationary view of a gravel parking area with parked cars, bollards, a small structure/kiosk, and trees; scene is essentially unchanged across all four frames indicating the ego vehicle is stopped.
per_camera.right: Views a garden area with ornamental grasses, a brick building with AC condenser units, and a person in a red jacket/garment near the units; scene is static across frames.
per_camera.rear: Interior garage/workshop view with a projector, whiteboard, shelving, traffic cones, and equipment; vehicle appears to be parked with rear facing into a garage bay, no motion.
per_camera.left: Shows a person in a green shirt leaning toward the vehicle window in the first frame, then a row of parked cars (Fiestas, sedans) in a gravel lot for subsequent frames; no vehicle motion apparent.

summary.overall: Ego vehicle is stationary in a parking/garage area with a person interacting near the left window; front_left camera is failed/overexposed.
summary.reason: Front_left camera is completely washed out (sensor failure or severe exposure fault) which is a notable data-quality anomaly; additionally a person is in close proximity to the left side of the vehicle. No erratic motion, but the camera fault merits flagging.

moments.label: Severe overexposure on front_left and front_right cameras

evidence.snippet: Frame almost entirely white; only faint blue/purple traces of foliage at left edge. No road geometry recoverable.
Heavily washed out; parked cars faintly visible on left and right, center of image fully saturated white.
Properly exposed: shows parking lot, trees, building at normal brightness.
Properly exposed: parked cars, gravel lot, trees normal.

moments.why_review: Front cameras are effectively blind for several seconds at the start of the window while side cameras expose correctly. Forward perception would be unavailable — critical for any AV driving decision. Suggests auto-exposure failure or ND mismatch on front sensors.
moments.inferred_ego_motion: stationary or very slow (scene barely changes on side cams)

moments.label: Recurrent front-camera overexposure after recovery

evidence.snippet: Frame again saturates to near-white with only faint trees/cars visible; after having been properly exposed moments earlier.
Also saturates white with foliage barely discernable.
Normal exposure, building and grass clearly visible.

moments.why_review: Second overexposure episode on both front cameras mid-window indicates repeated AE instability, not a one-off startup artifact. Needs investigation of exposure controller.

moments.label: Ego vehicle appears stationary for extended period

evidence.snippet: Scene of parking-lot entrance with barriers, booth, and parked cars is essentially unchanged across ~10 consecutive frames.
Garage interior with cones/bike is identical across many frames — no motion parallax.
Parked car line identical; no translation.

moments.why_review: Extended stall/idle — confirm whether vehicle is intentionally parked, waiting at a gate, or whether a planner stall occurred. Helps calibrate motion-from-video baseline since no telemetry exists.

moments.label: Person at close range to left side of vehicle (door-open scenario)

evidence.snippet: Bearded person in green jacket leaning in very close to the left side of the vehicle, head just outside/through the window frame; door pillar visible indicating door may be open.
Next left-camera frame shows parked cars directly with a door frame in foreground and no person — person has moved or door configuration changed.
Forward view shows parking lot normally; no indication of nearby person.

moments.why_review: Human at arm's length from the vehicle with an apparent open door suggests ingress/egress or operator interaction while the rig is recording. Safety-critical to confirm vehicle was not autonomously active at this moment; also tests whether other cameras should have observed the person.

moments.label: Rear camera shows indoor garage while other cameras show outdoor lot

evidence.snippet: Interior of a workshop/garage: ceiling tiles, whiteboards, toolbox, traffic cones, projector — clearly indoor environment framed by a vehicle's rear hatch.
Outdoor parking lot with trees and gate booth.
Outdoor building facade and grass.

moments.why_review: The rear camera is pointed into an indoor garage while front/side cameras see an outdoor lot. Either the vehicle is parked half-inside a garage opening, or the rear camera feed is mis-wired/stale. Important to verify camera-to-topic mapping and that rear feed is live.
moments.inferred_ego_motion: stationary, rear of vehicle inside/near garage opening

deep.rationale: Window is dominated by a stationary or near-stationary vehicle in a parking-lot/garage setting. Notable items are sensor-health issues rather than traffic events: (1) front_left and front_right cameras are severely overexposed at the start and again mid-window while side cameras expose normally, indicating an auto-exposure fault on the forward pair; (2) ego appears essentially static for most of the 20s window; (3) a person appears very close to the left side with an apparent open door near the end, suggesting human interaction with the rig; (4) the rear camera consistently shows a garage interior while other cameras show an outdoor lot, worth verifying as either physical positioning or a topic/mapping issue. No true near-misses, swerves, or sudden brake events were observed.

per_camera.front_left: Quiet residential street with parked cars on both sides, trees lining the road; ego moves forward slowly and approaches an intersection with a yellow-curbed corner. No pedestrians or dynamic hazards visible.
per_camera.front_right: Mirror view of the same residential street showing parked vehicles, trees, and a clear road ahead; forward progress is modest and steady through the intersection.
per_camera.right: Side view pans past parked cars, house facades, hedges, and a corner building marked '701'; consistent with slow forward travel, no close-margin hazards.
per_camera.rear: Rear view shows an empty residential street behind the ego (appears to be a bicycle-mounted camera given handlebars visible); no following vehicles or pedestrians.
per_camera.left: Left side shows parked cars, house gates, and trees slipping by; one parked dark SUV appears close but ego clears it without issue.

summary.overall: Routine low-speed traversal of a quiet residential street with parked cars and no dynamic actors.
summary.reason: No unusual road users, no close margins, clear weather, and no visual evidence of sudden braking or yaw; scene is uneventful.

per_camera.front_left: Ego vehicle is at an intersection near a graffiti-covered concrete wall with trees, then proceeds down a narrow semi-industrial street lined with walls and power poles; no other road users visible ahead.
per_camera.front_right: Similar forward view showing the same concrete wall/corner and then the street lined with walls, utility poles, and overhead wires; road is empty with scattered debris on the shoulders.
per_camera.right: Side view of a long graffiti-covered concrete wall, then a gated driveway and finally a billboard with a fast-food (pollo) ad; pedestrian sidewalk with litter, no people or vehicles close.
per_camera.rear: Rear views show the street behind with a parked car near a tree, then intersection and straight street receding; no following traffic, clear margin.
per_camera.left: Left side shows a residential/industrial area with a parked car across a lot, more walls with graffiti, and piles of litter/debris on the shoulder; no pedestrians or dynamic hazards.

summary.overall: Ego moves forward through a quiet semi-industrial alley between graffiti walls with no other road users.
summary.reason: Scene is uneventful: empty road, no pedestrians or vehicles near ego, steady forward motion with no visual signs of braking or sharp yaw.
